int.name: int
int.description: Sequence of digits without commas or decimals and optional sign character (ASCII characters "-" and "0" - "9" ). The sign character utilizes one byte (i.e. positive int is "99999" while negative int is "-99999"). Note that int values may contain leading zeros (e.g. "00023" = "23"). Examples: 723 in field 21 would be mapped int as |21=723|, -723 in field 12 would be mapped int as |12=-723|.

Length.name: Length
Length.description: Int field (see definition of "int" above) representing the length in bytes. Value must be positive.

NumInGroup.name: NumInGroup
NumInGroup.description: Int field (see definition of "int" above) representing the number of entries in a repeating group. Value must be positive.

SeqNum.name: SeqNum
SeqNum.description: Int field (see definition of "int" above) representing a message sequence number. Value must be positive.

TagNum.name: TagNum
TagNum.description: Int field (see definition of "int" above) representing a field's tag number when using FIX "Tag=Value" syntax. Value must be positive and may not contain leading zeros.

DayOfMonth.name: DayOfMonth
DayOfMonth.description: int field (see definition of "int" above) representing a day during a particular month (values 1-31).

float.name: float
float.description: Sequence of digits with optional decimal point and sign character (ASCII characters "-", "0" - "9" and "."); the absence of the decimal point within the string will be interpreted as the float representation of an integer value. All float fields must accommodate up to fifteen significant digits. The number of decimal places used should be a factor of business/market needs and mutual agreement between counterparties. Note that float values may contain leading zeros (e.g. "00023.23" = "23.23") and may contain or omit trailing zeros after the decimal point (e.g. "23.0" = "23.0000" = "23" = "23.").

Qty.name: Qty
Qty.description: Float field (see definition of "float" above) capable of storing either a whole number (no decimal places) of "shares" (securities denominated in whole units) or a decimal value containing decimal places for non-share quantity asset classes (securities denominated in fractional units).

Price.name: Price
Price.description: Float field (see definition of "float" above) representing a price. Note the number of decimal places may vary. For certain asset classes prices may be negative values. For example, options strategies can be negative under certain market conditions. Refer to Volume 7: FIX Usage by Product for asset classes that support negative price values.

PriceOffset.name: PriceOffset
PriceOffset.description: Float field (see definition of "float" above) representing a price offset, which can be mathematically added to a "Price". Note the number of decimal places may vary and some fields such as LastForwardPoints may be negative.

Amt.name: Amt
Amt.description: Float field (see definition of "float" above) typically representing a Price times a Qty.

Percentage.name: Percentage
Percentage.description: Float field (see definition of "float" above) representing a percentage (e.g. .05 represents 5% and .9525 represents 95.25%). Note the number of decimal places may vary.

char.name: char
char.description: Single character value, can include any alphanumeric character or punctuation except the delimiter. All char fields are case sensitive (i.e. m != M).

Boolean.name: Boolean
Boolean.description: Char field (see definition of "char" above) containing one of two values: 'Y' = True/Yes, 'N' = False/No.

String.name: String
String.description: Alpha-numeric free format strings, can include any character or punctuation except the delimiter. All char fields are case sensitive (i.e. morstatt != Morstatt).

MultipleValueString.name: MultipleValueString
MultipleValueString.description: String field (see definition of "String" above) containing one or more space delimited values.

Country.name: Country
Country.description: String field (see definition of "String" above) representing a country using ISO 3166 Country code (2 character) values.

Currency.name: Currency
Currency.description: String field (see definition of "String" above) representing a currency type using ISO 4217 Currency code (3 character) values.

Exchange.name: Exchange
Exchange.description: String field (see definition of "String" above) representing a market or exchange using ISO 10383 Market Identifier Code (MIC).

month-year.name: month-year
month-year.description: String field representing month of a year in YYYYMM format. An optional day of the month can be appended or an optional week code. Valid formats: YYYYMM YYYYMMDD YYYYMMWW. Valid values: YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31, WW = w1, w2, w3, w4, w5.

UTCTimestamp.name: UTCTimestamp
UTCTimestamp.description: Time/date combination represented in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") in either YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS (whole seconds) or YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss (milliseconds) format, colons, dash, and period required. Valid values: YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31, HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59, SS = 00-5960 (60 only if UTC leap second) (without milliseconds). YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31, HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59, SS = 00-5960 (60 only if UTC leap second), sss=000-999 (indicating milliseconds). Leap Seconds: Note that UTC includes corrections for leap seconds, which are inserted to account for slowing of the rotation of the earth. Leap second insertion is declared by the International Earth Rotation Service (IERS) and has, since 1972, only occurred on the night of Dec. 31 or Jun 30. The IERS considers March 31 and September 30 as secondary dates for leap second insertion, but has never utilized these dates. During a leap second insertion, a UTCTimestamp field may read "19981231-23:59:59", "19981231-23:59:60", "19990101-00:00:00". (see http://tycho.usno.navy.mil/leapsec.html )

UTCTimeOnly.name: UTCTimeOnly
UTCTimeOnly.description: Time-only represented in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") in either HH:MM:SS (whole seconds) or HH:MM:SS.sss (milliseconds) format, colons, and period required. This special-purpose field is paired with UTCDateOnly to form a proper UTCTimestamp for bandwidth-sensitive messages. Valid values: HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59, SS = 00-5960 (60 only if UTC leap second) (without milliseconds). HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59, SS = 00-5960 (60 only if UTC leap second), sss=000-999 (indicating milliseconds).

UTCDateOnly.name: UTCDateOnly
UTCDateOnly.description: Date represented in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") in YYYYMMDD format. This special-purpose field is paired with UTCTimeOnly to form a proper UTCTimestamp for bandwidth-sensitive messages. Valid values: YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31.

LocalMktDate.name: LocalMktDate
LocalMktDate.description: Date of Local Market (vs. UTC) in YYYYMMDD format. This is the "normal" date field used by the FIX protocol. Valid values: YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31.

data.name: data
data.description: Raw data with no format or content restrictions. Data fields are always immediately preceded by a length field. The length field should specify the number of bytes of the value of the data field (up to but not including the terminating SOH). Caution: the value of one of these fields may contain the delimiter (SOH) character. Note that the value specified for this field should be followed by the delimiter (SOH) character as all fields are terminated with an "SOH".

1.name: Account
1.description: Account mnemonic as agreed between buy and sell sides, e.g. broker and institution or investor/intermediary and fund manager.

2.name: AdvId
2.description: Unique identifier of Advertisement (7) message. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

3.name: AdvRefID
3.description: Reference identifier used with CANCEL and REPLACE transaction types. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

6.name: AvgPx
6.description: Calculated average price of all fills on this order. For Fixed Income trades AvgPx (6) is always expressed as percent-of-par, regardless of the PriceType (423) of LastPx (31) . I.e., AvgPx (6) will contain an average of percent-of-par values (see LastParPx (669) ) for issues traded in Yield, Spread or Discount.

7.name: BeginSeqNo
7.description: Message sequence number of first message in range to be resent

8.name: BeginString
8.description: Identifies beginning of new message and protocol version. ALWAYS FIRST FIELD IN MESSAGE. (Always unencrypted)

9.name: BodyLength
9.description: Message length, in bytes, is verified by counting the number of characters in the message following the BodyLength (9) field up to, and including, the delimiter immediately preceding the CheckSum (10) field. ALWAYS SECOND FIELD IN MESSAGE. (Always unencrypted) For example, for message 8=FIX 4.4^9=5^35=0^10=10^, the BodyLength is 5 for 35=0^

10.name: CheckSum
10.description: Three byte, simple checksum (see Volume 2: "Checksum Calculation" of FIX Specification for description). ALWAYS LAST FIELD IN MESSAGE; i.e. serves, with the trailing <SOH>, as the end-of-message delimiter. Always defined as three characters. (Always unencrypted)

11.name: ClOrdID
11.description: Unique identifier for Order as assigned by the buy-side (institution, broker, intermediary etc.) (identified by SenderCompID (49) or OnBehalfOfCompID (115) as appropriate). Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day. Firms, particularly those which electronically submit multi-day orders, trade globally or throughout market close periods, should ensure uniqueness across days, for example by embedding a date within the ClOrdID (11) field.

12.name: Commission
12.description: Commission. Note if CommType (13) is percentage, Commission of 5% should be represented as .05.

enumValues.name: PerUnit
enumValues.description: per unit (implying shares, par, currency, etc)

enumValues.name: PercentageWaivedCashDiscount
enumValues.description: (for CIV buy orders) percentage waived - cash discount

enumValues.name: PercentageWaivedEnhancedUnits
enumValues.description: (for CIV buy orders) percentage waived - enhanced units

enumValues.name: PointsPerBondOrOntractSupplyContractMultiplierInTheInstrumentComponentBlockIfTheObjectSecurityIsDenominatedInASizeOtherThanTheIndustryDefault1000ParForBonds
enumValues.description: points per bond or ontract [Supply ContractMultiplier (231) in the Instrument component block if the object security is denominated in a size other than the industry default - 1000 par for bonds.]

14.name: CumQty
14.description: Total quantity (e.g. number of shares) filled. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

15.name: Currency
15.description: Identifies currency used for price. Absence of this field is interpreted as the default for the security. It is recommended that systems provide the currency value whenever possible.

enumValues.name: CodesSpecificallyReservedForTestingPurposes
enumValues.description: Codes specifically reserved for testing purposes

enumValues.name: CodesAssignedForTransactionsWhereNoCurrencyIsInvolved
enumValues.description: Codes assigned for transactions where no currency is involved

enumValues.name: CodesSpecificallyReservedForTestingPurposes_1
enumValues.description: Codes specifically reserved for testing purposes

enumValues.name: CodesAssignedForTransactionsWhereNoCurrencyIsInvolved_1
enumValues.description: Codes assigned for transactions where no currency is involved

16.name: EndSeqNo
16.description: Message sequence number of last message in range to be resent. If request is for a single message BeginSeqNo (7) = EndSeqNo (16) . If request is for all messages subsequent to a particular message, EndSeqNo (16) = "0" (representing infinity).

17.name: ExecID
17.description: Unique identifier of Execution Report (8) message as assigned by sell-side (broker, exchange, ECN) (will be 0 (zero) for ExecType (150) =I (Order Status)). Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day or the life of a multi-day order. Firms which accept multi-day orders should consider embedding a date within the ExecID (17) field to assure uniqueness across days. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

18.name: ExecInst
18.description: Instructions for order handling on exchange trading floor. If more than one instruction is applicable to an order, this field can contain multiple instructions separated by space.

enumValues.name: PercentOfVolume
enumValues.description: Percent of volume (indicates that the sender does not want to be all of the volume on the floor vs. a specific percentage)

enumValues.name: ReinstateOnSystemFailure
enumValues.description: Reinstate on System Failure (mutually exclusive with Q)

enumValues.name: ReinstateOnTradingHalt
enumValues.description: Reinstate on Trading Halt (mutually exclusive with K)

enumValues.name: CancelOnTradingHalt
enumValues.description: Cancel on Trading Halt (mutually exclusive with J)

enumValues.name: CancelOnSystemFailure
enumValues.description: Cancel on System Failure (mutually exclusive with H)

enumValues.name: PrimaryPeg
enumValues.description: Primary peg (primary market - buy at bid/sell at offer)

19.name: ExecRefID
19.description: Reference identifier used with Trade Cancel and Trade Correct execution types. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

21.name: HandlInst
21.description: Instructions for order handling on Broker trading floor

enumValues.name: AutomatedExecutionOrderPrivateNoBrokerIntervention
enumValues.description: Automated execution order, private, no Broker intervention

enumValues.name: AutomatedExecutionOrderPublicBrokerInterventionOK
enumValues.description: Automated execution order, public, Broker intervention OK

22.name: SecurityIDSource
22.description: Identifies class or source of the SecurityID (48) value. Required if SecurityID (48) is specified. (formerly named: IDSource prior to FIX 4.3)

23.name: IOIid
23.description: Unique identifier of Indication of Interest (6) message. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

26.name: IOIRefID
26.description: Reference identifier used with CANCEL and REPLACE, transaction types. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

27.name: IOIQty
27.description: Quantity (e.g. number of shares) in numeric form or relative size. (formerly named: IOIShares prior to FIX 4.3)

28.name: IOITransType
28.description: Identifies Indication of Interest (6) message transaction type

30.name: LastMkt
30.description: Market of execution for last fill, or an indication of the market where an order was routed

32.name: LastQty
32.description: Quantity (e.g. shares) bought/sold on this (last) fill. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int) (formerly named: LastShares prior to FIX 4.3)

33.name: LinesOfText
33.description: Identifies number of lines of text body

35.name: MsgType
35.description: Defines message type. ALWAYS THIRD FIELD IN MESSAGE. (Always unencrypted) Note: A "U" as the first character in the MsgType (35) field (i.e. U1, U2, etc) indicates that the message format is privately defined between the sender and receiver.

37.name: OrderID
37.description: Unique identifier for Order as assigned by sell-side (broker, exchange, ECN). Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day. Firms which accept multi-day orders should consider embedding a date within the OrderID (37) field to assure uniqueness across days.

38.name: OrderQty
38.description: Quantity ordered. This represents the number of shares for equities or par, face or nominal value for FI instruments. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

enumValues.name: PendingCancel
enumValues.description: Pending Cancel (e.g. result of Order Cancel Request)

enumValues.name: PendingReplace
enumValues.description: Pending Replace (e.g. result of Order Cancel/Replace Request)

enumValues.name: Funari
enumValues.description: Funari (Limit Day Order with unexecuted portion handled as Market On Close. e.g. Japan)

enumValues.name: MarketWithLeftoverAsLimit
enumValues.description: Market with Leftover as Limit (market order then unexecuted quantity becomes limit order at last price)

enumValues.name: PreviousFundValuationPoint
enumValues.description: Previous Fund Valuation Point (Historic pricing) (for CIV)

enumValues.name: NextFundValuationPoint
enumValues.description: Next Fund Valuation Point (Forward pricing) (for CIV)

41.name: OrigClOrdID
41.description: ClOrdID (11) of the previous order (NOT the initial order of the day) as assigned by the institution, used to identify the previous order in cancel and cancel/replace requests.

42.name: OrigTime
42.description: Time of message origination (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT"))

43.name: PossDupFlag
43.description: Indicates possible retransmission of message with this sequence number

44.name: Price
44.description: Price per unit of quantity (e.g. per share)

48.name: SecurityID
48.description: Security identifier value of SecurityIDSource (22) type (e.g. CUSIP, SEDOL, ISIN, etc). Requires SecurityIDSource (22) .

49.name: SenderCompID
49.description: Assigned value used to identify firm sending message.

50.name: SenderSubID
50.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator (desk, trader, etc.)

52.name: SendingTime
52.description: Time of message transmission (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

53.name: Quantity
53.description: Overall/total quantity (e.g. number of shares) (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int) (formerly named: Shares prior to FIX 4.3)

54.name: Side
54.description: Side of order (see Volume 1: "Glossary" of FIX Specification for value definitions)

enumValues.name: Undisclosed
enumValues.description: Undisclosed (valid for IOI and List Order messages only)

enumValues.name: Cross
enumValues.description: Cross (orders where counterparty is an exchange, valid for all messages except IOIs)

55.name: Symbol
55.description: Ticker symbol. Common, "human understood" representation of the security. SecurityID (48) value can be specified if no symbol exists (e.g. non-exchange traded Collective Investment Vehicles). Use "[N/A]" for products which do not have a symbol.

56.name: TargetCompID
56.description: Assigned value used to identify receiving firm.

57.name: TargetSubID
57.description: Assigned value used to identify specific individual or unit intended to receive message. "ADMIN" reserved for administrative messages not intended for a specific user.

58.name: Text
58.description: Free format text string (Note: this field does not have a specified maximum length)

59.name: TimeInForce
59.description: Specifies how long the order remains in effect. Absence of this field is interpreted as DAY. NOTE not applicable to CIV Orders. (see Volume 1: "Glossary" for value definitions)

60.name: TransactTime
60.description: Time of execution/order creation (expressed in UTC - Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

62.name: ValidUntilTime
62.description: Indicates expiration time of indication message (always expressed in UTC - Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

63.name: SettlType
63.description: Indicates order settlement period. If present, SettlDate (64) overrides this field. If both SettlType (63) and SettlDate (64) are omitted, the default for SettlType (63) is 0 (Regular). Regular is defined as the default settlement period for the particular security on the exchange of execution. In Fixed Income the contents of this field may influence the instrument definition if the SecurityID (48) is ambiguous. In the US an active Treasury offering may be re-opened, and for a time one CUSIP will apply to both the current and "when-issued" securities. Supplying a value of "7" clarifies the instrument description; any other value or the absence of this field should cause the respondent to default to the active issue. (formerly named SettlmntTyp prior to FIX 4.4)

64.name: SettlDate
64.description: Specific date of trade settlement (SettlementDate) in YYYYMMDD format. If present, this field overrides SettlType (63) . This field is required if the value of SettlType (63) is 6 (Future) or 8 (Sellers Option). This field must be omitted if the value of SettlType (63) is 7 (When and If Issued) (expressed in local time at place of settlement) (formerly named FutSettDate prior to FIX 4.4)

65.name: SymbolSfx
65.description: Additional information about the security (e.g. preferred, warrants, etc.). Note also see SecurityType (167) .

66.name: ListID
66.description: Unique identifier for list as assigned by institution, used to associate multiple individual orders. Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day. Firms which generate multi-day orders should consider embedding a date within the ListID (66) field to assure uniqueness across days.

67.name: ListSeqNo
67.description: Sequence of individual order within list (i.e. ListSeqNo (67) of TotNoOrders (68) , 2 of 25, 3 of 25, . . . )

68.name: TotNoOrders
68.description: Total number of list order entries across all messages. Should be the sum of all NoOrders (73) in each message that has repeating list order entries related to the same ListID (66) . Used to support fragmentation. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was named "ListNoOrds")

69.name: ListExecInst
69.description: Free format text message containing list handling and execution instructions.

70.name: AllocID
70.description: Unique identifier for Allocation Instruction (J) message. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

72.name: RefAllocID
72.description: Reference identifier to be used with AllocTransType (71) = Replace or Cancel (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

73.name: NoOrders
73.description: Indicates number of orders to be combined for average pricing and allocation.

74.name: AvgPxPrecision
74.description: Indicates number of decimal places to be used for average pricing. Absence of this field indicates that default precision arranged by the broker/institution is to be used. (formerly named AvgPrxPrecision prior to FIX 4.4)

75.name: TradeDate
75.description: Indicates date of trade referenced in this message in YYYYMMDD format. Absence of this field indicates current day (expressed in local time at place of trade).

77.name: PositionEffect
77.description: Indicates whether the resulting position after a trade should be an opening position or closing position. Used for omnibus accounting - where accounts are held on a gross basis instead of being netted together. (formerly named: OpenClose prior to FIX 4.3)

78.name: NoAllocs
78.description: Number of repeating AllocAccount (79) / AllocPrice (366) entries.

80.name: AllocQty
80.description: Quantity to be allocated to specific sub-account (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int) (formerly named: AllocShares prior to FIX 4.3)

81.name: ProcessCode
81.description: Processing code for sub-account. Absence of this field in AllocAccount (79) / AllocPrice (366) / AllocQty (80) / ProcessCode (81) instance indicates regular trade.

82.name: NoRpts
82.description: Total number of reports within series.

83.name: RptSeq
83.description: Sequence number of message within report series.

84.name: CxlQty
84.description: Total quantity canceled for this order. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

85.name: NoDlvyInst
85.description: Number of delivery instruction fields to in repeating group. Note this field was removed in FIX 4.1 and reinstated in FIX 4.4.

93.name: SignatureLength
93.description: Number of bytes in signature field.

95.name: RawDataLength
95.description: Number of bytes in raw data field.

96.name: RawData
96.description: Unformatted raw data, can include bitmaps, word processor documents, etc.

97.name: PossResend
97.description: Indicates that message may contain information that has been sent under another sequence number.

enumValues.name: PGPDESMD5
enumValues.description: PGP/DES-MD5 (see app note on FIX web site)

enumValues.name: PEMDESMD5
enumValues.description: PEM/DES-MD5 (see app note on FIX web site)

99.name: StopPx
99.description: Price per unit of quantity (e.g. per share)

100.name: ExDestination
100.description: Execution destination as defined by institution when order is entered.

102.name: CxlRejReason
102.description: Code to identify reason for cancel rejection.

enumValues.name: OrderAlreadyInPendingCancelOrPendingReplaceStatus
enumValues.description: Order already in Pending Cancel or Pending Replace status

enumValues.name: UnableToProcessOrderMassCancelRequest
enumValues.description: Unable to process Order Mass Cancel Request (q)

enumValues.name: OrigOrdModTimeDidNotMatchLastTransactTimeOfOrder
enumValues.description: OrigOrdModTime (586) did not match last TransactTime (60) of order

103.name: OrdRejReason
103.description: Code to identify reason for order rejection. Note: Values 13, 14, and 15 will be used when rejecting an order due to pre-allocation information errors.

enumValues.name: DuplicateOfAVerballyCommunicatedOrder
enumValues.description: Duplicate of a verbally communicated order

104.name: IOIQualifier
104.description: Code to qualify Indication of Interest (6) use. (see Volume 1: "Glossary" for value definitions)

enumValues.name: AtTheClose
enumValues.description: At the close (around/not held to close)

enumValues.name: AtTheMarket
enumValues.description: At the Market (previously called Current Quote)

106.name: Issuer
106.description: Name of security issuer (e.g. International Business Machines, GNMA). See also Volume 7: "PRODUCT: FIXED INCOME - Euro Issuer Values" of FIX Specification .

110.name: MinQty
110.description: Minimum quantity of an order to be executed. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

111.name: MaxFloor
111.description: Maximum quantity (e.g. number of shares) within an order to be shown on the exchange floor at any given time. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

112.name: TestReqID
112.description: Identifier included in Test Request (1) message to be returned in resulting Heartbeat (0)

113.name: ReportToExch
113.description: Identifies party of trade responsible for exchange reporting.

enumValues.name: IndicatesThatPartyReceivingMessageMustReportTrade
enumValues.description: Indicates that party receiving message must report trade

enumValues.name: IndicatesThatPartySendingMessageWillReportTrade
enumValues.description: Indicates that party sending message will report trade

114.name: LocateReqd
114.description: Indicates whether the broker is to locate the stock in conjunction with a short sell order.

enumValues.name: IndicatesTheBrokerIsResponsibleForLocatingTheStock
enumValues.description: Indicates the broker is responsible for locating the stock

enumValues.name: IndicatesTheBrokerIsNotRequiredToLocate
enumValues.description: Indicates the broker is not required to locate

115.name: OnBehalfOfCompID
115.description: Assigned value used to identify firm originating message if the message was delivered by a third party i.e. the third party firm identifier would be delivered in the SenderCompID (49) field and the firm originating the message in this field.

116.name: OnBehalfOfSubID
116.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator (i.e. trader) if the message was delivered by a third party

118.name: NetMoney
118.description: Total amount due as the result of the transaction (e.g. for Buy order - principal + commission + fees) reported in currency of execution.

119.name: SettlCurrAmt
119.description: Total amount due expressed in settlement currency (includes the effect of the forex transaction)

enumValues.name: CodesSpecificallyReservedForTestingPurposes
enumValues.description: Codes specifically reserved for testing purposes

enumValues.name: CodesAssignedForTransactionsWhereNoCurrencyIsInvolved
enumValues.description: Codes assigned for transactions where no currency is involved

enumValues.name: CodesSpecificallyReservedForTestingPurposes_1
enumValues.description: Codes specifically reserved for testing purposes

enumValues.name: CodesAssignedForTransactionsWhereNoCurrencyIsInvolved_1
enumValues.description: Codes assigned for transactions where no currency is involved

121.name: ForexReq
121.description: Indicates request for forex accommodation trade to be executed along with security transaction.

enumValues.name: DoNotExecuteForexAfterSecurityTrade
enumValues.description: Do not execute Forex after security trade

122.name: OrigSendingTime
122.description: Original time of message transmission (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") when transmitting orders as the result of a resend request.

123.name: GapFillFlag
123.description: Indicates that the Sequence Reset (4) message is replacing administrative or application messages which will not be resent.

enumValues.name: GapFillMessageMsgSeqNumFieldValid
enumValues.description: Gap Fill message, MsgSeqNum (34) field valid

124.name: NoExecs
124.description: No of execution repeating group entries to follow.

126.name: ExpireTime
126.description: Time/Date of order expiration (always expressed in UTC - Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") The meaning of expiration is specific to the context where the field is used. For orders, this is the expiration time of a Good Til Date TimeInForce. For Quotes - this is the expiration of the quote. Expiration time is provided across the quote message dialog to control the length of time of the overall quoting process. For collateral requests, this is the time by which collateral must be assigned. For collateral assignments, this is the time by which a response to the assignment is expected.

128.name: DeliverToCompID
128.description: Assigned value used to identify the firm targeted to receive the message if the message is delivered by a third party i.e. the third party firm identifier would be delivered in the TargetCompID (56) field and the ultimate receiver firm ID in this field.

129.name: DeliverToSubID
129.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message recipient (i.e. trader) if the message is delivered by a third party

130.name: IOINaturalFlag
130.description: Indicates that Indication of Interest (6) is the result of an existing agency order or a facilitation position resulting from an agency order, not from principal trading or order solicitation activity.

134.name: BidSize
134.description: Quantity of bid (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

135.name: OfferSize
135.description: Quantity of offer (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

136.name: NoMiscFees
136.description: Number of repeating groups of miscellaneous fees

enumValues.name: CodesSpecificallyReservedForTestingPurposes
enumValues.description: Codes specifically reserved for testing purposes

enumValues.description: Codes assigned for transactions where no currency is involved

enumValues.name: CodesSpecificallyReservedForTestingPurposes_1
enumValues.description: Codes specifically reserved for testing purposes

enumValues.name: CodesAssignedForTransactionsWhereNoCurrencyIsInvolved_1
enumValues.description: Codes assigned for transactions where no currency is involved

141.name: ResetSeqNumFlag
141.description: Indicates that the both sides of the FIX session should reset sequence numbers.

142.name: SenderLocationID
142.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator's location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader)

143.name: TargetLocationID
143.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message destination's location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader)

144.name: OnBehalfOfLocationID
144.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator's location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader) if the message was delivered by a third party

145.name: DeliverToLocationID
145.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message recipient's location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader) if the message was delivered by a third party

146.name: NoRelatedSym
146.description: Specifies the number of repeating symbols specified.

147.name: Subject
147.description: The subject of an Email (C) message

148.name: Headline
148.description: The headline of a News (B) message

149.name: URLLink
149.description: A URI (Uniform Resource Identifier) or URL (Uniform Resource Locator) link to additional information (i.e. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uniform_Resource_Locator ). See " Appendix 6-B FIX Fields Based Upon Other Standards " of FIX.

150.name: ExecType
150.description: Describes the specific Execution Report (8) (i.e. Pending Cancel) while OrdStatus (39) will always identify the current order status (i.e. Partially Filled)

enumValues.name: PendingCancel
enumValues.description: Pending Cancel (e.g. result of Order Cancel Request (F) )

enumValues.name: RestatedSentUnsolicitedBySellsideWithExecRestatementReasonSet
enumValues.description: Restated ( ExecutionRpt (8) sent unsolicited by sellside, with ExecRestatementReason (378) set)

enumValues.name: PendingReplace
enumValues.description: Pending Replace (e.g. result of Order Cancel/Replace Request (G) )

151.name: LeavesQty
151.description: Quantity open for further execution. If the OrdStatus (39) is Canceled, DoneForTheDay, Expired, Calculated, or Rejected (in which case the order is no longer active) then LeavesQty (151) could be 0, otherwise LeavesQty (151) = OrderQty (38) - CumQty (14) . (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

152.name: CashOrderQty
152.description: Specifies the approximate order quantity desired in total monetary units vs. as tradeable units (e.g. number of shares). The broker or fund manager (for CIV orders) would be responsible for converting and calculating a tradeable unit (e.g. share) quantity ( OrderQty (38) ) based upon this amount to be used for the actual order and subsequent messages.

153.name: AllocAvgPx
153.description: AvgPx (6) for a specific AllocAccount (79) . For Fixed Income this is always expressed as "percent of par" price type.

155.name: SettlCurrFxRate
155.description: Foreign exchange rate used to compute SettlCurrAmt (119) from Currency (15) to SettlCurrency (120)

156.name: SettlCurrFxRateCalc
156.description: Specifies whether or not SettlCurrFxRate (155) should be multiplied or divided.

157.name: NumDaysInterest
157.description: Number of Days of Interest for convertible bonds and fixed income. Note value may be negative.

158.name: AccruedInterestRate
158.description: The amount the buyer compensates the seller for the portion of the next coupon interest payment the seller has earned but will not receive from the issuer because the issuer will send the next coupon payment to the buyer. Accrued Interest Rate is the annualized Accrued Interest amount divided by the purchase price of the bond.

159.name: AccruedInterestAmt
159.description: Amount of Accrued Interest for convertible bonds and fixed income

160.name: SettlInstMode
160.description: Indicates mode used for Settlement Instructions (T) message.

enumValues.name: SpecificOrderForASingleAccount
enumValues.description: Specific Order for a single account (for CIV)

161.name: AllocText
161.description: Free format text related to a specific AllocAccount (79) .

enumValues.name: Restate
enumValues.description: Restate (used where the Settlement Instruction is being used to communicate standing instructions which have not been changed or added to)

164.name: EmailThreadID
164.description: Unique identifier for an email thread (new and chain of replies)

167.name: SecurityType
167.description: Indicates type of security. See also the Product (460) and CFICode (461) fields. It is recommended that CFICode (461) be used instead of SecurityType (167) for non-Fixed Income instruments.

168.name: EffectiveTime
168.description: Time the details within the message should take effect (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated), also known as "GMT")

169.name: StandInstDbType
169.description: Identifies the Standing Instruction database used

enumValues.name: AGlobalCustodianMustBeProvided
enumValues.description: A Global Custodian ( StandInstDbName (170) must be provided)

170.name: StandInstDbName
170.description: Name of the Standing Instruction database represented with StandInstDbType (169) (i.e. the Global Custodian's name).

171.name: StandInstDbID
171.description: Unique identifier used on the Standing Instructions database for the Standing Instructions to be referenced.

enumValues.name: VersusPaymentDeliverOrReceiveVsPayment
enumValues.description: "Versus. Payment": Deliver (if Sell) or Receive (if Buy) vs. (Against) Payment

enumValues.name: FreeDeliverOrReceiveFree
enumValues.description: "Free": Deliver (if Sell) or Receive (if Buy) Free

189.name: BidForwardPoints
189.description: Bid F/X forward points added to spot rate. May be a negative value.

191.name: OfferForwardPoints
191.description: Offer F/X forward points added to spot rate. May be a negative value.

192.name: OrderQty2
192.description: OrderQty (38) of the future part of a F/X swap order.

193.name: SettDate2
193.description: SettlDate (64) of the future part of a F/X swap order. (formerly named FutSettDate2)

195.name: LastForwardPoints
195.description: F/X forward points added to LastSpotRate (194) . May be a negative value.

196.name: AllocLinkID
196.description: Can be used to link two different Allocation Instruction (J) messages (each with unique AllocID (70) ) together, i.e. for F/X "Netting" or "Swaps". Should be unique.

197.name: AllocLinkType
197.description: Identifies the type of Allocation Instruction (J) linkage when AllocLinkID (196) is used.

198.name: SecondaryOrderID
198.description: Assigned by the party which accepts the order. Can be used to provide the OrderID (37) used by an exchange or executing system.

199.name: NoIOIQualifiers
199.description: Number of repeating groups of IOIQualifiers.

200.name: MaturityMonthYear
200.description: Can be used with standardized derivatives vs. the MaturityDate (541) field. Month and Year of the maturity (used for standardized futures and options). Format: YYYYMM (i.e. 199903) YYYYMMDD (20030323) YYYYMMwN (200303w1) for week A specific date or can be appended to the MaturityMonthYear (200) . For instance, if multiple standard products exist that mature in the same Year and Month, but actually mature at a different time, a value can be appended, such as "w1" or "w2" to indicate week 1 as opposed to week 2 expiration. Likewise, the date (01-31) can be appended to indicate a specific expiration (maturity date).

203.name: CoveredOrUncovered
203.description: Used for derivative products, such as options

206.name: OptAttribute
206.description: Can be used for SecurityType (167) =OPT to identify a particular security. Valid values vary by SecurityExchange: For Exchanges: DTB (Frankfurt), HKSE (Hong Kong), and SOFFEX (Zurich) 0-9 = single digit "version" number assigned by exchange following capital adjustments (0=current, 1=prior, 2=prior to 1, etc).

207.name: SecurityExchange
207.description: Market used to help identify a security.

208.name: NotifyBrokerOfCredit
208.description: Indicates whether or not details should be communicated to BrokerOfCredit (i.e. step-in broker).

209.name: AllocHandlInst
209.description: Indicates how the receiver (i.e. third party) of Allocation Instruction (J) message should handle/process the account details.

210.name: MaxShow
210.description: Maximum quantity (e.g. number of shares) within an order to be shown to other customers (i.e. sent via an Indication of Interest (6) ). (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

211.name: PegOffsetValue
211.description: Amount (signed) added to the peg for a pegged order in the context of the PegOffsetType (836) (Prior to FIX 4.4 this field was of type PriceOffset) (formerly named PegDifference prior to FIX 4.4)

212.name: XmlDataLen
212.description: Length of the XmlData (213) data block.

213.name: XmlData
213.description: Actual XML data stream (e.g. FIXML). See appropriate XML reference (e.g. FIXML). Note: may contain embedded SOH characters.

214.name: SettlInstRefID
214.description: Reference identifier for the SettlInstID (162) with Cancel and Replace SettlInstTransType (163) transaction types.

215.name: NoRoutingIDs
215.description: Number of repeating groups of RoutingID (217) and RoutingType (216) values. See Volume 3: "Pre-Trade Message Targeting/Routing" of FIX Specification

216.name: RoutingType
216.description: Indicates the type of RoutingID (217) specified.

217.name: RoutingID
217.description: Assigned value used to identify a specific routing destination.

218.name: Spread
218.description: For Fixed Income. Either Swap Spread or Spread to Benchmark depending upon the order type. Spread to Benchmark: Basis points relative to a benchmark. To be expressed as "count of basis points" (vs. an absolute value). E.g. High Grade Corporate Bonds may express price as basis points relative to benchmark (the BenchmarkCurveName (221) field). Note: Basis points can be negative. Swap Spread: Target spread for a swap. (formerly named: SpreadToBenchmark prior to FIX 4.3)

220.name: BenchmarkCurveCurrency
220.description: Identifies currency used for benchmark curve. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

enumValues.name: CodesSpecificallyReservedForTestingPurposes
enumValues.description: Codes specifically reserved for testing purposes

enumValues.description: Codes assigned for transactions where no currency is involved

enumValues.description: Codes specifically reserved for testing purposes

enumValues.name: CodesAssignedForTransactionsWhereNoCurrencyIsInvolved_1
enumValues.description: Codes assigned for transactions where no currency is involved

221.name: BenchmarkCurveName
221.description: Name of benchmark curve. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

222.name: BenchmarkCurvePoint
222.description: Point on benchmark curve. Free form values: e.g. "1Y", "7Y", "INTERPOLATED". Sample values: 1M combination of a number between 1-12 and a "M" for month 1Y combination of number between 1-100 and a "Y" for year 10Y-OLD see above, then add "-OLD" when appropriate INTERPOLATED the point is mathematically derived 2/2031 5 3/8 the point is stated via a combination of maturity month / year and coupon See Fixed Income-specific documentation at http://www.fixtrading.org for additional values. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

223.name: CouponRate
223.description: The rate of interest that, when multiplied by the principal, par value, or face value of a bond, provides the currency amount of the periodic interest payment. The coupon is always cited, along with maturity, in any quotation of a bond's price.

224.name: CouponPaymentDate
224.description: Date interest is to be paid. Used in identifying Corporate Bond issues. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

225.name: IssueDate
225.description: The date on which a bond or stock offering is issued. It may or may not be the same as the effective date ("Dated Date") or the date on which interest begins to accrue ("Interest Accrual Date") (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

226.name: RepurchaseTerm
226.description: Number of business days before repurchase of a repo. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (Deprecated)

227.name: RepurchaseRate
227.description: Percent of par at which a Repo will be repaid. Represented as a percent, e.g. .9525 represents 95-1/4 percent of par. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (Deprecated)

228.name: Factor
228.description: For Fixed Income: Amorization Factor for deriving Current face from Original face for ABS or MBS securities, note the fraction may be greater than, equal to or less than 1. In TIPS securities this is the Inflation index. Qty * Factor * Price = Gross Trade Amount For Derivatives: Contract Value Factor by which price must be adjusted to determine the true nominal value of one futures/options contract. (Qty * Price) * Factor = Nominal Value (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

229.name: TradeOriginationDate
229.description: Used with Fixed Income for Muncipal New Issue Market. Agreement in principal between counter-parties prior to actual trade date. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

230.name: ExDate
230.description: The date when a distribution of interest is deducted from a securities assets or set aside for payment to bondholders. On the ex-date, the securities price drops by the amount of the distribution (plus or minus any market activity). (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

231.name: ContractMultiplier
231.description: Specifies the ratio or multiply factor to convert from "nominal" units (e.g. contracts) to total units (e.g. shares) (e.g. 1.0, 100, 1000, etc). Applicable For Fixed Income, Convertible Bonds, Derivatives, etc. In general quantities for all classes should be expressed in the basic unit of the instrument, e.g. shares for equities, norminal or par amount for bonds, currency for foreign exchange. When quantity is expressed in contracts, e.g. financing transactions and bond trade reporting, ContractMultiplier (231) should contain the number of units in one contract and can be omitted if the multiplier is the default amount for the instrument, i.e. 1,000 par of bonds, 1,000,000 par for financing transactions.

232.name: NoStipulations
232.description: Number of stipulation entries (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3).

233.name: StipulationType
233.description: For Fixed Income. Type of Stipulation or Prepayment Speeds. Other types may be used by mutual agreement of the counterparties. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

234.name: StipulationValue
234.description: For Fixed Income. Value of stipulation. The expression can be an absolute single value or a combination of values and logical operators: < value > value <= value >= value value value1 - value2 value1 OR value2 value1 AND value2 YES NO Bargain conditions recognized by the London Stock Exchange - to be used when StipulationType (233) is "BGNCON". CD Special cum Dividend XD Special ex Dividend CC Special cum Coupon XC Special ex Coupon CB Special cum Bonus XB Special ex Bonus CR Special cum Rights XR Special ex Rights CP Special cum Capital Repayments XP Special ex Capital Repayments CS Cash Settlement SP Special Price TR Report for European Equity Market Securities in accordance with Chapter 8 of the Rules GD Guaranteed Delivery Values for StipulationType (233) = "PXSOURCE": BB GENERIC BB FAIRVALUE BROKERTEC ESPEED GOVPX HILLIARD FARBER ICAP TRADEWEB TULLETT LIBERTY If a particular side of the market is wanted append /BID /OFFER or /MID. plus appropriate combinations of the above and other expressions by mutual agreement of the counterparties. Examples: ">=60", ".25", "ORANGE OR CONTRACOSTA", etc. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

235.name: YieldType
235.description: Type of yield. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

236.name: Yield
236.description: Yield percentage. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

237.name: TotalTakedown
237.description: The price at which the securities are distributed to the different members of an underwriting group for the primary market in Municipals, total gross underwriter's spread. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

238.name: Concession
238.description: Provides the reduction in price for the secondary market in Muncipals. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

239.name: RepoCollateralSecurityType
239.description: Identifies the collateral used in the transaction. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (Deprecated)

240.name: RedemptionDate
240.description: Return of investor's principal in a security. Bond redemption can occur before maturity date. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (Deprecated)

241.name: UnderlyingCouponPaymentDate
241.description: Underlying security's CouponPaymentDate. See CouponPaymentDate (224) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

242.name: UnderlyingIssueDate
242.description: Underlying security's IssueDate. See IssueDate (225) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

243.name: UnderlyingRepoCollateralSecurityType
243.description: Underlying security's RepoCollateralSecurityType. See RepoCollateralSecurityType (239) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (Deprecated)

244.name: UnderlyingRepurchaseTerm
244.description: Underlying security's RepurchaseTerm. See RepurchaseTerm (226) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (Deprecated)

245.name: UnderlyingRepurchaseRate
245.description: Underlying security's RepurchaseRate. See RepurchaseRate (227) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (Deprecated)

246.name: UnderlyingFactor
246.description: Underlying security's Factor. See Factor (228) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

247.name: UnderlyingRedemptionDate
247.description: Underlying security's RedemptionDate. See RedemptionDate (240) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate) (Deprecated)

248.name: LegCouponPaymentDate
248.description: Multileg instrument's individual leg security's CouponPaymentDate. See CouponPaymentDate (224) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

249.name: LegIssueDate
249.description: Multileg instrument's individual leg security's IssueDate. See IssueDate (225) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

250.name: LegRepoCollateralSecurityType
250.description: Multileg instrument's individual leg security's RepoCollateralSecurityType. See RepoCollateralSecurityType (239) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (Deprecated)

251.name: LegRepurchaseTerm
251.description: Multileg instrument's individual leg security's RepurchaseTerm. See RepurchaseTerm (226) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (Deprecated)

252.name: LegRepurchaseRate
252.description: Multileg instrument's individual leg security's RepurchaseRate. See RepurchaseRate (227) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (Deprecated)

253.name: LegFactor
253.description: Multileg instrument's individual leg security's Factor. See Factor (228) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

254.name: LegRedemptionDate
254.description: Multileg instrument's individual leg security's RedemptionDate. See RedemptionDate (240) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate) (Deprecated)

255.name: CreditRating
255.description: An evaluation of a company's ability to repay obligations or its likelihood of not defaulting. These evaluation are provided by Credit Rating Agencies, i.e. S&P, Moody's. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

256.name: UnderlyingCreditRating
256.description: Underlying security's CreditRating. See CreditRating (255) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

257.name: LegCreditRating
257.description: Multileg instrument's individual leg security's CreditRating. See CreditRating (255) field for description (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

258.name: TradedFlatSwitch
258.description: Driver and part of trade in the event that the Security Master file was wrong at the point of entry (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

259.name: BasisFeatureDate
259.description: BasisFeatureDate (259) allows requesting firms within fixed income the ability to request an alternative yield-to-worst, -maturity, -extended or other call. This flows through the confirm process. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

260.name: BasisFeaturePrice
260.description: Price for BasisFeatureDate. See BasisFeatureDate (259) (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

262.name: MDReqID
262.description: Unique identifier for Market Data Request (V)

264.name: MarketDepth
264.description: Depth of market for Book Snapshot

265.name: MDUpdateType
265.description: Specifies the type of Market Data update.

266.name: AggregatedBook
266.description: Specifies whether or not book entries should be aggregated. Broker option if not specified.

enumValues.name: OneBookEntryPerSidePerPrice
enumValues.description: one book entry per side per price

enumValues.name: MultipleEntriesPerSidePerPriceAllowedBrokerOption
enumValues.description: Multiple entries per side per price allowed(Not specified) = broker option

268.name: NoMDEntries
268.description: Number of entries in Market Data message.

270.name: MDEntryPx
270.description: Price of the Market Data Entry.

271.name: MDEntrySize
271.description: Quantity or volume represented by the Market Data Entry.

272.name: MDEntryDate
272.description: Date of Market Data Entry. (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

276.name: QuoteCondition
276.description: Space-delimited list of conditions describing a quote.

277.name: TradeCondition
277.description: Space-delimited list of conditions describing a trade

enumValues.name: StoppedStock
enumValues.description: Stopped Stock (guarantee of price but does not execute the order)

enumValues.name: ImbalanceMoreBuyers
enumValues.description: Imbalance More Buyers (Cannot be used in combination with Q)

enumValues.name: ImbalanceMoreSellers
enumValues.description: Imbalance More Sellers (Cannot be used in combination with P)

279.name: MDUpdateAction
279.description: Type of Market Data update action.

281.name: MDReqRejReason
281.description: Reason for the rejection of a Market Data Request (V) .

282.name: MDEntryOriginator
282.description: Originator of a Market Data Entry

283.name: LocationID
283.description: Identification of a Market Maker's location

284.name: DeskID
284.description: Identification of a Market Maker's desk

286.name: OpenCloseSettlFlag
286.description: Flag that identifies a market data entry. (Prior to FIX 4.3 this field was of type char) (formerly named OpenCloseSettleFlag prior to FIX 4.4)

287.name: SellerDays
287.description: Specifies the number of days that may elapse before delivery of the security

290.name: MDEntryPositionNo
290.description: Display position of a bid or offer, numbered from most competitive to least competitive, per market side, beginning with 1.

292.name: CorporateAction
292.description: Identifies the type of Corporate Action.

295.name: NoQuoteEntries
295.description: The number of quote entries for a QuoteSet.

296.name: NoQuoteSets
296.description: The number of sets of quotes in the message.

297.name: QuoteStatus
297.description: Identifies the status of the quote acknowledgement. (formerly named: QuoteAckStatus prior to FIX 4.3)

298.name: QuoteCancelType
298.description: Identifies the type of quote cancel.

299.name: QuoteEntryID
299.description: Uniquely identifies the quote as part of a QuoteSet.

301.name: QuoteResponseLevel
301.description: Level of Response requested from receiver of quote messages.

enumValues.name: AcknowledgeOnlyNegativeOrErroneousQuotes
enumValues.description: Acknowledge only negative or erroneous quotes

303.name: QuoteRequestType
303.description: Indicates the type of Quote Request (R) being generated

304.name: TotNoQuoteEntries
304.description: Total number of quotes for the QuoteSet across all messages. Should be the sum of all NoQuoteEntries (295) in each message that has repeating quotes that are part of the same QuoteSet. (Prior to FIX 4.4 this field was named TotQuoteEntries)

305.name: UnderlyingSecurityIDSource
305.description: Underlying security's SecurityIDSource. See SecurityIDSource (22) field for description (formerly named: UnderlyingIDSource prior to FIX 4.3)

306.name: UnderlyingIssuer
306.description: Underlying security's Issuer. See Issuer (106) field for description

307.name: UnderlyingSecurityDesc
307.description: Underlying security's SecurityDesc. See SecurityDesc (107) field for description

308.name: UnderlyingSecurityExchange
308.description: Underlying security's SecurityExchange (207) . Can be used to identify the underlying security.

309.name: UnderlyingSecurityID
309.description: Underlying security's SecurityID. See SecurityID (48) field for description

310.name: UnderlyingSecurityType
310.description: Underlying security's SecurityType (167) . The following applies when used in conjunction with SecurityType (167) =REPO Represents the general or specific type of security that underlies a financing agreement Valid values for SecurityType (167) =REPO: TREASURY Federal government or treasury PROVINCE State, province, region, etc. AGENCY Federal agency MORTGAGE Mortgage passthrough CP Commercial paper CORP Corporate EQUITY Equity SUPRA Supra-national agency CASH If bonds of a particular issuer or country are wanted in an Order or are in the basket of an Execution and the SecurityType (167) is not granular enough, include the UnderlyingIssuer (306) , UnderlyingCountryOfIssue (592) , UnderlyingProgram, UnderlyingRegType and/or UnderlyingStipulations block e.g.: SecurityType=REPO UnderlyingSecurityType=MORTGAGE UnderlyingIssuer=GNMA or SecurityType=REPO UnderlyingSecurityType=AGENCY UnderlyingIssuer=CA Housing Trust UnderlyingCountryOfIssue=CA or SecurityType=REPO UnderlyingSecurityType=CORP UnderlyingNoStipulations=1 UnderlyingStipulationType=RATING UnderlyingStipulationValue=>bbb-

311.name: UnderlyingSymbol
311.description: Underlying security's Symbol. See Symbol (55) field for description

312.name: UnderlyingSymbolSfx
312.description: Underlying security's SymbolSfx. See SymbolSfx (65) field for description

313.name: UnderlyingMaturityMonthYear
313.description: Underlying security's MaturityMonthYear. Can be used with standardized derivatives vs. the UnderlyingMaturityDate (542) field. See MaturityMonthYear (200) field for description

316.name: UnderlyingStrikePrice
316.description: Underlying security's StrikePrice. See StrikePrice (202) field for description

317.name: UnderlyingOptAttribute
317.description: Underlying security's OptAttribute. See OptAttribute (206) field for description

318.name: UnderlyingCurrency
318.description: Underlying security's Currency. See Currency (15) field for description

enumValues.name: PerUnit

enumValues.name: VariableCabinetTradePrice

enumValues.description: Exchange/switch CIV order - Buy driven, cash top-up (i.e. additional cash will be provided to fulfil the order)

enumValues.name: ExchangeSwitchCIVOrderBuyDrivenCashWithdraw

enumValues.description: Order Origination Trader (associated with Order Origination Firm - e.g. trader who initiates/submits the order)

459.description: Identifies class or source of the UnderlyingSecurityAltID (458) value. Required if UnderlyingSecurityAltID (458) is specified.

460.description: Indicates the type of product the security is associated with. See also the CFICode (461) and SecurityType (167) fields.

461.name: CFICode
461.description: Indicates the type of security using ISO 10962 standard, Classification of Financial Instruments (CFI code) values. ISO 10962 is maintained by ANNA (Association of National Numbering Agencies) acting as Registration Authority. See also the Product (460) and SecurityType (167) fields. It is recommended that CFICode (461) be used instead of SecurityType (167) for non-Fixed Income instruments. A subset of possible values applicable to FIX usage are identified in " Appendix 6-D CFICode Usage - ISO 10962 Classification of Financial Instruments (CFI code) "

463.description: Underlying security's CFICode. See CFICode (461) field description

464.description: Indicates whether or not this FIX Session is a "test" vs. "production" connection. Useful for preventing "accidents".

465.description: Designates the type of quantities (e.g. OrderQty (38) ) specified. Used for MBS and TIPS Fixed Income security types. (Deprecated)

466.name: BookingRefID
466.description: Common reference passed to a post-trade booking process (e.g. industry matching utility).

467.name: IndividualAllocID
467.description: Unique identifier for a specific NoAllocs (78) repeating group instance (e.g. for an AllocAccount (79) ).

468.name: RoundingDirection
468.description: Specifies which direction to round For CIV - indicates whether or not the quantity of shares/units is to be rounded and in which direction where CashOrderQty (152) or (for CIV only) OrderPercent (516) are specified on an order. The default is for rounding to be at the discretion of the executing broker or fund manager. e.g. for an order specifying CashOrderQty (152) or OrderPercent (516) if the calculated number of shares/units was 325.76 and RoundingModulus (469) was 10 - "round down" would give 320 units, "round up" would give 330 units and "round to nearest" would give 320 units.

469.name: RoundingModulus
469.description: For CIV - a float value indicating the value to which rounding is required. i.e. 10 means round to a multiple of 10 units/shares; 0.5 means round to a multiple of 0.5 units/shares. The default, if RoundingDirection (468) is specified without RoundingModulus (469) , is to round to a whole unit/share.

470.name: CountryOfIssue

537.name: QuoteType
537.description: Identifies the type of quote. An indicative quote is used to inform a counterparty of a market. An indicative quote does not result directly in a trade. A tradeable quote is submitted to a market and willresult directly in a trade against other orders and quotes in a market. A restricted tradeable quote is submitted to a market and within a certain restriction (possibly based upon price or quantity) will automatically trade against orders. Order that do not comply with restrictions are sent to the quote issuer who can choose to accept or decline the order. A counter quote is used in the negotiation model. See Volume 7 - Product: Fixed Income for example usage.

549.description: Type of cross being submitted to a market

enumValues.name: CrossTradeWhichIsExecutedCompletelyOrNotBothSidesAreTreatedInTheSameMannerThisIsEquivalentToAnAllOrNone
enumValues.description: Cross Trade which is executed completely or not. Both sides are treated in the same manner. This is equivalent to an All or None.

enumValues.name: CrossTradeWhichIsExecutedPartiallyAndTheRestIsCancelledOneSideIsFullyExecutedTheOtherSideIsPartiallyExecutedWithTheRemainderBeingCancelledThisIsEquivalentToAnImmediateOrCancelOnTheOtherSideNoteTheCrossPrioritizationFieldMayBeUsedToIndicateWhichSideShouldFullyExecuteInThisScenario
enumValues.description: Cross Trade which is executed partially and the rest is cancelled. One side is fully executed, the other side is partially executed with the remainder being cancelled. This is equivalent to an Immediate or Cancel on the other side. Note: The CrossPrioritization (550) field may be used to indicate which side should fully execute in this scenario.

enumValues.name: CrossTradeWhichIsPartiallyExecutedWithTheUnfilledPortionsRemainingActiveOneSideOfTheCrossIsFullyExecutedFieldButTheUnfilledPortionRemainsActive
enumValues.description: Cross trade which is partially executed with the unfilled portions remaining active. One side of the cross is fully executed (as denoted with the CrossPrioritization (550) field), but the unfilled portion remains active.

enumValues.name: CrossTradeIsExecutedWithExistingOrdersWithTheSamePriceInTheCaseOtherOrdersExistWithTheSamePriceTheQuantityOfTheCrossIsExecutedAgainstTheExistingOrdersAndQuotesTheRemainderOfTheCrossIsExecutedAgainstTheOtherSideOfTheCrossTheTwoSidesPotentiallyHaveDifferentQuantities
enumValues.description: Cross trade is executed with existing orders with the same price. In the case other orders exist with the same price, the quantity of the Cross is executed against the existing orders and quotes, the remainder of the cross is executed against the other side of the cross. The two sides potentially have different quantities.

550.name: CrossPrioritization
550.description: Indicates if one side or the other of a cross order should be prioritized. The definition of prioritization is left to the market. In some markets prioritization means which side of the cross order is applied to the market first. In other markets - prioritization may mean that the prioritized side is fully executed (sometimes referred to as the side being protected).
